summary.intent: Address Codex review findings on PR #710 for the GridList component, fixing five contract violations (disabled root tab order, Space/Replace behavior, disabled link hrefs, typeahead timestamp accumulation, and roleless cell aria-selected in list mode), then pass all local CI gates and push the fix.
summary.outcome: All five Codex findings were resolved with implementation patches, aligned spec updates, and regression tests. The branch coverage threshold was initially missed and required three rounds of targeted test additions before the gate passed. The commit was pushed to impl-285 and a new @codex review triggered.

repo: The project uses `cargo xcov` as a native coverage gate with a 90.0% branch threshold for ars-components; the threshold is exact, not rounded, so a metric that displays as 90.0% can still fail.

Snapshot acceptance uses `cargo insta accept --workspace` since the filtered `--snapshot <name>` flag does not match the generated full identifier and silently skips; `-p` flag is not supported on `cargo insta accept`.
The typeahead timestamp convention is `last_key_time + 1ms` (not `+ TYPEAHEAD_TIMEOUT`); this is established in ars-collections helpers and must be matched in GridList for spec alignment.
Space in Replace selection-behavior mode should dispatch `Select(key)` not `ToggleSelect(key)`; the reducer applies the correct selection-mode constraints from there.
Non-composite GridList cells must not carry `aria-selected`; only composite/row-role cells in list mode should have it.

The spec file is at `spec/components/data-display/grid-list.md` and is validated via `cargo xtask spec validate`; anatomy conformance is checked via `cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance`.

The nightly llvm-cov binary lives at `~/.rustup/toolchains/nightly-aarch64-apple-darwin/lib/rustlib/aarch64-apple-darwin/bin/llvm-cov` and must be invoked directly since `rustup which --toolchain nightly llvm-cov` returns nothing on this machine.
The coverage binary hash changes on each `cargo xcov` rebuild, requiring a fresh `find target/llvm-cov-target/debug/deps` lookup before running `llvm-cov show`.

code.finding: Root element tab order and disabled state attrs; disabled root must not receive tabindex=0.
Keyboard dispatch helper; Space key must dispatch Select (not ToggleSelect) when selection_behavior is Replace.
Typeahead fallback timestamp must use last_key_time + 1ms, not last_key_time + TYPEAHEAD_TIMEOUT.
RTL ArrowRight guard branch and Escape clear-selection arm were uncovered by existing tests.
DnD attrs: `dnd_enabled == true` with a disabled cell (the `&& !disabled` false branch) was uncovered.
row_attrs: selection_mode=None path and pointer-origin focus (non-keyboard-visible) false branch were uncovered.
Snapshot updated to remove invalid aria-selected from non-composite roleless cell; intentional regression.

workflow: Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment via `gh api repos/.../issues/.../comments`; readiness is polled by checking for the eyes reaction and counting unresolved threads authored by codex.
The pattern for polling CI + Codex is: reactions check → GraphQL unresolved-threads count → `gh pr checks`; all three run in parallel before each sleep interval.
`cargo +nightly fmt` must be run after any `if let` chain edits; the formatter reindents these even when manually written correctly.
When `apply_patch` fails due to shifted context, the correct recovery is to `sed -n` the exact current line range and retry with a smaller, precisely-anchored hunk.
Spec and implementation must be updated in the same commit to avoid spec/impl drift flagged by future reviews.

Running `cargo llvm-cov test` for the whole package produces too much text; scope with `-- data_display::grid_list` and use `llvm-cov show` with `-show-branches=count` and `sed` to slice the relevant line ranges.

friction: `cargo insta accept -p ars-components` fails silently; `-p` is not supported and `--snapshot <name>` does not match the full generated identifier — only `--workspace` reliably accepts pending snapshots.
`cargo xcov` rebuilds the full instrumented test binary on every invocation, making each coverage iteration 5-10 minutes of wall-clock wait.
The llvm-cov binary hash changes on each rebuild, invalidating the path used in `llvm-cov show` commands and requiring a fresh `find` lookup each time.
The coverage threshold failure message does not indicate which branches are missed; annotated `llvm-cov show` output must be sliced manually with `sed` to locate uncovered regions.
`apply_patch` failed on the first attempt for every multi-hunk patch because surrounding test assertions had shifted; required splitting into single-location hunks.
Three separate coverage iterations (RTL/Escape branches → DnD disabled branch → row_attrs/focus-visible branch) were needed before the exact branch percentage cleared the threshold.
The `@codex review` trigger and polling loop required many sleep intervals (60s, 120s, 180s) with no early-exit mechanism when Codex is still in progress.
open_items: Codex has not yet posted its thumbs-up on the follow-up push; a new review poll cycle is needed after CI completes on the latest commit.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label was planned to be reapplied after the push but was not confirmed as applied in the transcript.
The defensive missing-cell-id branch identified in coverage analysis was noted but not explicitly tested; it may still be an uncovered path.
Coverage is passing now but only by a narrow margin; any future branch additions to GridList will likely require accompanying tests to stay above the 90.0% floor.
